articles.title: Sensex falls over 680 pts, Nifty slips below 24,200 mark as crude oil spike weighs, FMCGs, oil & gas stocks drag
articles.description: Benchmark equity indices Sensex and Nifty tumbled on Wednesday amid renewed US-Iran tensions and a fresh spike in crude oil prices.

articles.title: Commodities: Oil Bounces On Persian Gulf Re-Escalation
articles.description: Re-escalation in the Persian Gulf has reignited supply concerns, pushing oil prices higher amid questions about the direction of US-Iran peace talks. Read more here.

articles.title: Rupee slips as oil prices jump, modest portfolio inflows cushion fall
articles.description: The Indian rupee weakened slightly on Wednesday due to Middle East hostilities. Renewed conflict lifted oil prices, impacting risk sentiment across markets. Modest portfolio inflows helped cushion the rupee's downward pressure. Elevated oil prices pose a risk to India's economic growth and inflation. Investors also await U.S. Federal Reserve policy meeting minutes later today.

articles.title: Nitin Gadkari Defends E20 Fuel Policy, Challenges Critics To Show Vehicle Damage
articles.description: Union Minister Nitin Gadkari defended India’s E20 ethanol-blended petrol programme amid criticism over mileage concerns and vehicle issues. He challenged critics to name a single car damaged by E20 fuel, while highlighting the policy’s role in reducing crude oil imports, saving foreign exchange and cutting carbon emissions

articles.title: Oil surges after U.S. launches strikes on Iran
articles.description: U.S. military strikes on Iran send oil prices surging after attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, threatening a fragile ceasefire and raising inflation concerns ahead of the midterm elections.

articles.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Asian shares were mixed on Wednesday and oil prices surged more than 3% after the U.S. launched strikes on Iran following attacks on three ships…

articles.title: Oil Prices Jump After Iran Attacks Commercial Vessels
articles.description: Renewed U.S.-Iran military strikes and attacks on commercial shipping near the Strait of Hormuz have lifted oil prices and reminded markets that geopolitical risks remain a major threat to global energy supplies.
